description: Clean positive controls — abstracts with direct biomedical evidence aligned with one hypothesis. Guards against the prompt becoming so cautious that it labels everything 'neither'.
hypothesis_1: Autism is caused by Genetic predisposition.

items.title: De novo CHD8 loss-of-function variants in autism
items.abstract_excerpt: Whole-exome sequencing across 2,500 trios identifies de novo loss-of-function variants in CHD8 significantly enriched in autism probands relative to unaffected siblings (OR 12.5, p < 1e-6).
items.why: Direct genetic evidence in autism cases — aligns with 'autism is caused by genetic predisposition'.

items.title: Heritability of autism spectrum disorder in a Swedish twin cohort
items.abstract_excerpt: MZ-DZ twin concordance analysis estimates ASD heritability at 0.83 (95% CI 0.79-0.87), with shared environmental factors contributing negligibly.
items.why: Quantitative heritability evidence; direct biomedical evidence that genetic factors cause ASD.

items.title: Vaccine-coverage rates in autism families: a retrospective cohort
items.abstract_excerpt: We compared vaccination coverage between 1,200 children with autism and 4,800 matched controls. Coverage was modestly lower in autism families (76% vs 81%, p = 0.02); no difference in vaccine timing was observed.
items.why: Mentions both autism and vaccines but reports coverage rates, not causation. Does not bear on either hypothesis's causal claim.

items.title: Population-based study finds no association between MMR vaccination and autism risk
items.abstract_excerpt: Cohort of 657,461 children; HR for ASD after MMR vaccination = 0.93 (95% CI 0.85-1.02). Stratification by autism susceptibility, age, and post-vaccination interval showed no signal.
items.why: Direct evidence AGAINST H2 (vaccines cause autism). The schema has no 'refutes' label, so refute-shaped evidence for H2 lands in 'neither' (it also does not provide evidence for H1).
